channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: Ukraine’s drones are spoiling Putin’s vision for Crimea
item.dc:creator: Mary Ilyushina, David Stern, Natalia Abbakumova
item.description: Russian-installed authorities in Crimea have halted fuel distribution in vacation season as Ukraine isolates the peninsula.

item.title: Trump dismisses Iran’s rejection of nuclear inspections
item.dc:creator: Adam Taylor, Victoria Craw
item.description: Iran’s Foreign Ministry said there was no plan for the IAEA to inspect its damaged nuclear facilities, a day after Vice President JD Vance said conversations with inspectors could happen imminently.

item.title: Keir Starmer is out. Other establishment politicians are in trouble.
item.dc:creator: Steve Hendrix
item.description: Across the West’s advanced democracies, conventional political parties are being gutted by stagnant wages and fraying social contracts that have left voters fed up with the status quo.

item.title: U.S. shifts its Latin America approach. Here’s how it’s playing out in Guatemala.
item.dc:creator: Karen DeYoung, Samantha Schmidt
item.description: U.S. lobbyists and pundits with President Donald Trump’s ear are aiding Latin America’s swing to the right.

item.title: Who is Andy Burnham, U.K.’s ‘King of the North’ and likely next prime minister?
item.dc:creator: Karla Adam, Steve Hendrix
item.description: The runaway favorite to succeed Prime Minister Keir Starmer is a longtime mayor equally comfortable in the corridors of Westminster and in working-class northern England.

item.title: U.K. Prime Minister Starmer to resign as Labour Party seeks reboot
item.dc:creator: Greg Miller, Steve Hendrix
item.description: The party’s crushing losses in local elections had triggered a mutiny against Starmer. Andy Burnham, the mayor of Greater Manchester, emerged as a heavy favorite to be the next PM.

item.title: Read Keir Starmer’s resignation speech
item.dc:creator: Washington Post staff
item.description: Here is the text of the British prime minister's resignation speech outside 10 Downing Street on Monday. It set the stage for a new Labour Party leader to become government leader by September.

item.title: High-level U.S.-Iran talks conclude with a road map for peace, mediators say
item.dc:creator: Mary Ilyushina, Sammy Westfall, Susannah George
item.description: The negotiations were shaken by President Donald Trump’s threats of fresh attacks, but Pakistan and Qatar hailed “encouraging progress.”
